newsSinceJun6: Only routine corporate news: the June 9, 2026 AGM in Jena confirmed the previously proposed dividend of EUR 0.40/share (vs EUR 0.38 prior year) and elected three supervisory board members (Andreas Krey, Dr Frank Stietz new), with ~60% of capital represented. No new contracts, guidance, M&A, capacity announcements, or notable analyst moves since June 6. The material drivers - Q1 order intake +75% YoY to EUR 357M with semiconductor & advanced manufacturing orders at EUR 180M vs EUR 69M, and the CEO transition (Traeger out Feb 2026, Dominik Dorfner appointed in March) - all predate the window. Stock drifted -0.73% today; 17-analyst consensus target (~EUR 32) sits well below the ~EUR 44 price after a +115% YTD run.

sources.label: Jenoptik press release - Results of the 2026 AGM (Jun 9, 2026)
stocksguide - Jenoptik analyst consensus and price targets
optics.org - Jenoptik reports strong orders in opening quarter (Q1 2026)
Jenoptik IR - Dr. Stefan Traeger leaves Jenoptik (Nov 2025, context only)

verdicts.rankRationale: Hold at rank 41 on the scaling list: today's AGM (dividend confirmation, board seats) is pure governance housekeeping and the -0.73% day is drift, so there is no genuinely new information versus yesterday's hold. Slight -1 for rerating discipline - a +115% YTD double with no fresh fundamental evidence since June 6 and a ~EUR 32 consensus target vs ~EUR 44 price keeps compressing residual alpha.
verdicts.bull: Semiconductor & advanced manufacturing orders nearly tripled YoY in Q1 (EUR 180M vs EUR 69M) on lithography and inspection optics, and the Jena semiconductor-optics capacity expansion positions Jenoptik as a scarce qualified supplier in the EUV/inspection optics chain at only ~$2.9B market cap.
verdicts.neutral: Thesis intact but fully discovered: the stock has doubled YTD on the known order surge, the AGM changed nothing, and the next real datapoint is H1 results plus new CEO Dorfner's strategic agenda - until then the name marks time.
verdicts.bear: Price sits ~35-40% above the 17-analyst consensus target; any softening in semicap optics orders, or a strategy reset by the incoming CEO, would hit a name now priced for sustained order momentum, while non-photonics legacy segments dilute the AI-optics purity.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: H1 2026 results / Q2 order intake confirming the semiconductor-optics order surge is sustained, plus first strategic signals from new CEO Dominik Dorfner.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Post-doubling derating toward the ~EUR 32 consensus target if semicap equipment order momentum stalls or the lithography/inspection capex cycle pauses.
